sections.heading: Conservation of Momentum Calculator: What It Is and How to Use It
sections.content: A Conservation of Momentum Calculator is a free online physics tool that helps you calculate final velocities or unknown momentum values in collisions. Using the law of conservation of momentum, it provides fast, accurate results without needing to do complex calculations by hand.

subSections.heading: Open the Calculator
subSections.content: Access it directly in your browser.

subSections.heading: Enter Masses
subSections.content: Input the masses of the colliding objects (m₁ and m₂).

subSections.heading: Enter Initial Velocities
subSections.content: Add the velocities before collision (u₁ and u₂).

subSections.heading: Select Collision Type
subSections.content: Choose elastic, inelastic, or generic if available.

subSections.heading: Enter Known Final Velocity
subSections.content: Leave the unknown variable blank; the calculator will solve for it.

subSections.heading: Click Calculate
subSections.content: Instantly see final velocities or the unknown momentum.

subSections.heading: Check Units
subSections.content: Make sure all units match the system you are using.

subSections.heading: Instant Results
subSections.content: Get calculations immediately without manual formulas.

subSections.heading: Multiple Outputs
subSections.content: Compute final velocities, total momentum, or unknown variables.

subSections.heading: Mobile-Friendly
subSections.content: Works on phones, tablets, and desktops.

subSections.heading: Free & Easy to Use
subSections.content: No downloads or registration required.

subSections.heading: Educational
subSections.content: Helps understand momentum and collision concepts clearly.

subSections.heading: Supports Metric & Imperial Units
subSections.content: Flexible for users worldwide.

subSections.heading: Students & Teachers
subSections.content: Solve homework, verify classroom problems, or demonstrate concepts.

subSections.heading: Physics Enthusiasts
subSections.content: Experiment with different mass and velocity combinations.

subSections.heading: Hobbyists & Game Designers
subSections.content: Simulate realistic collisions for animations or games.

subSections.heading: Scenario Analysis
subSections.content: Compare multiple setups to see how momentum is conserved.

subSections.heading: Educational Demonstrations
subSections.content: Illustrate the law of momentum conservation clearly.

subSections.heading: Example 1 – Elastic Collision
subSections.content: Mass m₁: 2 kg | Velocity u₁: 5 m/s | Mass m₂: 3 kg | Velocity u₂: -2 m/s | Final Velocities: v₁ = -1 m/s, v₂ = 4 m/s

subSections.heading: Example 2 – Inelastic Collision
subSections.content: Mass m₁: 4 kg | Velocity u₁: 3 m/s | Mass m₂: 2 kg | Velocity u₂: 1 m/s | Final Combined Velocity: 2.33 m/s

faq.q: What is a Conservation of Momentum Calculator?
faq.a: It calculates final velocities or unknown momentum values in collisions using the law of momentum conservation.
Yes, it is completely free to use online.

faq.q: Do I need software or installation?
faq.a: No, it runs directly in your browser.

faq.q: Can I use it on mobile devices?

faq.a: Masses, initial velocities, collision type, and optionally one final velocity.
It relies on the law of conservation of momentum for isolated systems.
Yes, it’s ideal for learning, homework, and exams.

faq.q: Can it handle different collision types?
faq.a: Yes, elastic, inelastic, and generic collisions are supported.
Yes, both metric and imperial units are supported.
It provides approximate results for ideal situations; real-world collisions may vary due to energy loss, friction, or external forces.
